title: Data Analyst (Marketing Analytics & Performance Insights)
description:
About the Role



We are looking for a Data Analyst who can turn marketing and customer service data into clear, actionable insights that drive real results.



You’ll work closely with law firm clients and internal teams to analyze performance, optimize campaigns, and improve customer interactions. This role is perfect for someone who enjoys combining data, strategy, and client impact. Not just reporting numbers, but influencing decisions.



What You’ll Do

Analyze & Validate Data

Collect, clean, and validate data from marketing platforms and customer service systems

Ensure high data accuracy and consistency across reports

Identify trends, patterns, and performance drivers



Build Reports & Deliver Insights

Create dashboards and reports using tools like Google Analytics, Tableau, Power BI, and internal systems

Translate complex data into simple, actionable recommendations

Deliver insights that clients and teams can actually implement



Audit & Optimize Marketing Campaigns

Evaluate digital campaigns (Google Ads, Meta, etc.)

Identify optimization opportunities to improve ROI and performance

Present clear recommendations and best practices

Support training sessions for internal teams and clients



Improve Customer Experience

Analyze call data and customer interactions

Identify gaps affecting lead quality, conversions, and satisfaction

Work with QA teams to improve call handling and response times



Collaborate & Lead

Work closely with marketing, customer service, and strategy teams

Support and guide junior analysts and QA auditors

Help turn insights into execution-ready action plans



Work Directly with Clients

Understand client goals and performance challenges

Deliver data-driven recommendations tailored to their growth

Track and communicate measurable improvements





What Success Looks Like

High accuracy in data and reporting

Insights that are actually implemented (not ignored)

Measurable improvements in campaign performance and customer service metrics

Strong collaboration across teams and with clients



What We’re Looking For

Core Skills

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

Ability to translate data into clear business insights

Experience with marketing analytics and performance tracking

Excellent communication and presentation skills



Technical Skills

Google Analytics (GA4 preferred)

Excel / Google Sheets (advanced level)

Data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, or similar)

Experience with CRM and call tracking data is a plus





Experience

2–5+ years in data analytics, marketing analytics, or similar role

Experience working with performance marketing data (paid ads, funnels, etc.)

Experience working with clients is a strong advantage



Important Note

This role is focused on impact, not just reporting. We’re looking for someone who doesn’t just analyze data but uses it to drive decisions and results.





Include the following in your application: Put Purple in the subject line.

Your updated resume

One example of a data-driven project or analysis (include the problem, your approach, insights, and results)

A sample dashboard or report you’ve created

description:

processing inbound deal flow

researching companies and contacts

Review deals from brokers, marketplaces, and inbound
s

Prepare structured summaries (“dossiers”)

Draft personalized outreach
s using templates

Ensure nothing falls through the cracks

Maintain and update contact lists

What This Role Is NOT

Not a creative marketing role

Not a social media/content role

Not a passive “task execution” role



This is an operations + research role with judgment



What We Are Looking For

Must-Have

Ability to follow structured processes

Comfortable using tools like Google Docs, spreadsheets, CRM systems

Nice to Have

Experience with research, lead generation, or CRM systems

Experience supporting outbound
 campaigns

Full-time role

How to Apply

Why you are a good fit for this role

This role requires:



precision

consistency

strong thinking



If you prefer unstructured or creative work without clear guidelines, this role will not be a fit.



Final Note



The goal of this role is to help build a high-performance sourcing and outreach system.



If you are detail-oriented, structured, and want to take ownership of meaningful work, this can be a strong long-term opportunity.

title: Webflow Designer for Service-Based Websites
description:
Are you a Webflow designer who leans more editorial, refined, and minimal than techy and heavy?



We’re looking for a designer to support ongoing client projects.

Our work is strategy-led, clean, and conversion-focused, with a strong emphasis on white space, hierarchy, and restraint.



This is not a role for “just build it” developers.

We care about how things feel, not just how they function.



THE TYPE OF WORK WE DO



We design websites for service-based businesses.



Think

Clean layouts

Strong hierarchy

Generous white space

Refined typography

Subtle interactions

Elevated, considered design



Not

Dark, heavy, tech-style builds

Over-designed layouts

Cluttered sections or excessive effects



If your portfolio leans corporate, SaaS, or developer-heavy, this likely isn’t the right fit.



WHY YOU’LL ENJOY WORKING WITH US



Clear direction. Strategy, copy, and wireframes are provided

Defined scope. No guesswork, no vague briefs

Flexible hours. Work to deadlines, not fixed shifts

Respectful collaboration. No last-minute chaos

Long-term partnership. Consistent work for the right person



We prioritise quality over speed.



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING



Build websites in Webflow based on provided strategy and wireframes

Translate brand direction into clean, high-end layouts

Structure CMS collections logically

Apply SEO basics during build

Refine and improve existing Webflow sites

Ensure all builds are easy for clients to manage



Optional



Design in Figma before build



REQUIRED SKILLS



WEBFLOW

Strong experience building in Webflow

Confident with CMS, components, and interactions

Clean builds only. No messy overrides or shortcuts



CLIENT-FIRST

Solid understanding of Client-First structure

Clear class naming and scalable systems



SEO BASICS

Proper heading structure

Meta titles and descriptions

Alt text and CMS SEO fields

Internal linking



THE NON-NEGOTIABLE



Your work must demonstrate

Strong use of white space

Clean, minimal layouts

High-end, service-based design

Thoughtful typography



We will not consider portfolios that



Feel overly masculine, tech-heavy, or corporate

Are cluttered or visually noisy

Prioritise effects over clarity



YOUR WORKING STYLE



You work well independently with clear direction

You communicate early if something’s off

You meet deadlines

You’re open to feedback

You take pride in clean, considered work



HOURS AND RATE



Part-time, ongoing

Flexible hours

Work to deadlines, not fixed shifts

Rate based on experience



HOW TO APPLY



2 to 4 live Webflow sites you personally built

At least one clean, service-based website

An example using Client-First

A short note on how you approach SEO in Webflow

Your hourly rate and weekly availability



Applications without relevant examples will not be reviewed.

title: Automotive Glass Sales Agent B2C
description:
Fix.Glass is a dynamic, customer-focused company in the vehicle glass replacement industry. We are looking for motivated Internal Sales Agents to join our remote team! You will handle high-volume inbound sales leads, answer and make calls, send messages, book jobs, and order parts and above all manage client expectations! If you thrive in a fast-paced environment and have excellent communication and negotiation skills, we want to hear from you!







Key Responsibilities:



Handle inbound and outbound sales calls professionally.

Respond to customer inquiries via phone and messaging platforms on our all in one customized platform which helps you in sales to keep perfect track over every single lead.

Process job bookings and coordinate part orders.

Convert leads into successful sales using negotiation and problem-solving skills.

Maintain accurate records and meet sales targets.

Ensure bookings are attended to and all communication and services are managed between all parties.

Order parts from our suppliers.

Able to work in a fast paced environment, all leads have a short life cycle.

Work Schedule And Compensation:

You will work under one of the following shift patterns - UK time 08:00am to 20:00pm

Choose your shift type:



Shift type 1: 3 days on, 1 day off (11 hour shift +1 hour lunch) 110 hours over two weeks  



Shift type 2: 3 days on, 2 days off (11 hour shift +1 hour lunch) 88 hours over two weeks



28 Paid Holidays accumulated per calendar year. We do not follow weekdays or national holidays. Holidays must be chosen manually yourself, and you can match them with national holidays if you wish.

Probation period is 6 months, during this time you can accumulate paid leave but can only use it on public holidays within your Country.

Payments made Wednesday every two weeks straight into your bank account.

Salary: Paid per hour £4.40, you will need to make more commission than hours worked to get a good production rate.





Requirements: ?



A quiet home workspace with a professional setup.

A PC and dual monitor, wired microphone, and cable fiber internet connection for seamless communication (WIFI not recommended).

Strong sales, negotiation, and problem-solving skills.

Self-motivated, goal-oriented, and able to work independently.

PC and technical knowledge is a must.

Attention to detail.

Why Join Fix.Glass?



Remote Work: Enjoy the freedom of working from the comfort of your home.

Innovative Environment: Be part of a forward-thinking company with a passion for excellence.

Supportive Team: Work alongside a collaborative team dedicated to your success.

On average our top Sales agent makes £800 - £1000+ or more every two weeks if you push to succeed!

About Winston Gray

Winston Gray is a boutique business transformation firm working with founders, CEOs, and leadership teams navigating growth, complexity, and change. Our work re-creates and re-defines businesses by creating operational efficiencies, removing bottlenecks and streamlining processes. 



Winston Gray is one of the fastest growing business transformation firms in Australia, working with medium to extra large commercial companies. 



The baseline of marketing at Winston Gray is to harmonies the work we do by building digital authority. We do not market to chase leads, trends, or algorithms.



The Role

We are looking for a sharp, structured Marketing Manager to run our authority-led content engine. This role is execution-focused, detail-oriented, and requires strong editorial judgement.



You will work closely with the Director, translating her thinking, voice notes, and strategic direction into high-quality written and visual content across LinkedIn, SEO articles and the website.



This is not a social media role.

This is not a growth hacking role.

This is a credibility and content operations role.



Key Responsibilities

Content & LinkedIn

• Draft LinkedIn posts for both the Founder (primary) and Winston Gray (secondary)

• Work within clearly defined content pillars and post types

• Manage scheduling, formatting, and content tracking

• Maintain a clean, organised content library



SEO & Articles

• Use Google Trends and search analysis to identify ownable topics

• Draft long-form articles aligned to senior, AFR-style editorial standards

• Ensure articles are intentional, well-structured, and optimised (not keyword dumping)

• Maintain internal linking and article hygiene



Video Support

• Light editing of 1 founder-led video per week

• Add captions and basic formatting

• No heavy effects, trends, or overproduction



Website Hygiene

• Keep blog and website content current and aligned to positioning

• Make light copy updates as needed

• Ensure consistency across channels



Reporting & Coordination

• Prepare weekly content summaries and upcoming schedules

• Flag decisions or approvals required

• Track what is drafted, approved, live, and in backlog



This role is not to:

• chase trends

• post daily

• write tips-and-tricks content

• run paid ads or lead funnels

• optimise for vanity metrics



Required Experience

• Understanding and experience of the Key Responsibilities (above)

• Strong written English (non-negotiable)

• Background in B2B content, consulting, finance, legal, or professional services preferred

• Experience working from draft notes and senior input

• Strong editorial judgement and ability to maintain tone

• Comfortable working asynchronously with clear standards



What Good Looks Like

• Content sounds calm, senior, and credible

• Founder voice is preserved, not diluted

• Marketing plans are created and executed on time 

• Marketing is structured, consistent to tone and brand

• Content is disciplined, not busy or fluffy

• Written formats are refined, meaningful and clear

title: Sales Lead (Construction)
description:
Sales Representative — Electrical Construction Bid Operations



About the Role

You'll own the full bid lifecycle across two (and growing) electrical contractors — from submission through contract close. This is a phone-heavy, pipeline-driven role where your follow-up directly translates to revenue. Every bid gets tracked, every GC relationship gets worked, and nothing goes cold on your watch.



What You'll Do

Manage the live bid board daily. Every morning starts with reviewing what's pending, what needs follow-up, and what's close to winning.

Make 100+ outbound calls daily to GC project managers, estimators, and decision-makers. These are warm calls — we've already submitted a proposal. You're following up to learn where we stand, gauge competitor pricing, handle scope questions, and push toward award.

Run a disciplined follow-up cadence. Every bid gets touched at 3, 7, and 14 days. Every touchpoint is logged in the CRM with pricing intel, GC preferences, and timeline updates.

Conduct outreach to get our contractors on new bid lists. Cold and warm outreach to general contractors doing commercial retail and restaurant work in the LA market. Goal: more bid invitations every week.

Gather competitive intelligence. When a GC says "you were high" or "we went with someone else," you log it, spot pricing patterns, and flag where a small adjustment could flip a loss into a win.

Feed intel back to the estimating team. If you're hearing we're consistently high on a certain project type, you bring that data back so we can recalibrate before the next bid goes out.

Track win/loss metrics weekly: follow-up rate, response rate, deals moved to negotiation, contracts closed, and revenue attributed to your efforts.



Requirements

Strong English communication. You'll be calling American GCs who expect fluent, confident, real conversation — no scripts.

Sales or outbound calling experience (minimum 2 year). Inside sales, SDR/BDR, appointment setting, or BPO backgrounds all work.



Available during US Pacific Time hours. Most GC offices are reachable 7 AM–4 PM PT. You need meaningful overlap with that window.



CRM discipline. Every call logged, every status updated, no exceptions. If it's not in the system, it didn't happen.

Self-directed. You'll have daily targets but nobody hovering. You manage your own call list, prioritize the highest-value follow-ups, and flag problems before they become missed deals.



Comfortable with rejection. GCs are busy, often blunt, and sometimes don't return calls. Persistence without being pushy is the skill.



Coachable and curious. You'll be learning electrical construction terminology, project types, and GC relationship dynamics. The person who asks questions and absorbs fast will succeed here.



Strong Preference

Construction industry experience — especially electrical contracting, commercial buildouts, or tenant improvement work. If you've worked on the GC or sub side and understand how bids flow, scope gets negotiated, and awards get made, you'll ramp significantly faster and have an immediate edge.



Nice-to-Haves

Experience with bid platforms like BuildingConnected or PlanHub. Familiarity with CRM tools (
, HubSpot, or similar). Bilingual English/Spanish — valuable in the LA construction market. Any real estate or trades industry exposure.

title: Long-Form AI Video Editor + AI Storytelling Producer (Character Consistency)
description:
Type: Contract / Part-time (room to grow into full-time)



Remote: Yes



Start: ASAP



Tools: We provide the AI tools and accounts



(IMPORTANT: MUST SUBMIT A PORTFOLIO OF AI WORKS)



About the Role



We’re hiring a long-form AI video storyteller who can produce narrative-driven videos using AI generation tools and strong editing instincts. This role is NOT short-form reels—it’s for long-form storytelling (target 10–15 minute videos: YouTube-length narratives, episodic content, cinematic sequences).



You should be able to create videos that feel cohesive and intentional: consistent characters, consistent environments, consistent visual style, and strong pacing from start to finish.



We provide access to the AI tools. What we need from you is craft, taste, and storytelling and proven experience making AI-generated video content. We will provide all scripts for the projects.





What You’ll Do

- Produce long-form AI videos from concept to final export (with our direction).



- Maintain character consistency across scenes (face, body, wardrobe, style, mannerisms).



- Maintain visual consistency (lighting, color palette, camera language, environment continuity).



- Build scenes using AI tools and refine outputs until they match the story requirements.



- Assemble full edits: pacing, transitions, music, sound design, dialogue/VO, SFX.



- Work with our provided scripts and elevate them through visuals and editing choices.



- Create and manage a repeatable workflow for episodes (templates, style bibles, prompt systems).



- Organize assets and versions cleanly for handoffs and future iterations.





Key Skills We Need (Must-Have)



Long-form storytelling editing(structure, tension, payoffs, emotional rhythm).



Strong understanding of cinematic language (shots, continuity, pacing, coverage).



Proven ability to create and maintain:

- Consistent characters

-Consistent art direction

-Consistent locations / world-building



Experience with AI video generation pipelines and iteration workflows.



Clean workflow habits (file organization, naming, version control mindset).





AI Experience (What Your Portfolio Should Demonstrate)



We’re specifically looking for creators who already have AI projects showing:



Character continuity across multiple scenes.



Style consistency across an entire video (or episode).



Cohesive world-building (settings, props, clothing, time of day).



Ability to troubleshoot AI artifacts and keep quality high.



We do not require any specific AI toolssince we’ll provide the stack—what matters is that you understand the workflow and can execute.



Editing Tools



Premiere / DaVinci / After Effects (any is fine).



Strong audio skills are a big plus (dialogue clarity, music mixing, SFX layers).



Bonus: experience building pipelines (asset libraries, prompt libraries, shot lists).





What Success Looks Like



You can take a script/outline and deliver a cohesive long-form AI video.



Characters stay recognizable and stable from scene to scene.



Visuals feel like one connected world — not random AI clips stitched together.



The final video feels intentional, emotional, and watchable all the way through.

----------------------------------------------------

What to Send When Applying (Required)



Portfolio links (AI long-form preferred).



1–2 examples that specifically show character consistency across multiple scenes.



A short breakdown of your workflow (how you keep characters/visuals consistent).



Your rate (per minute of finished video OR per project) and weekly availability.



Bonus: Include a short “breakdown reel” showing prompts, iterations, and before/after improvements.





Nice-to-Haves



Episodic storytelling experience (series structure).



Shot listing / storyboarding ability.



Prompt systems / “style bible” creation.



Team collaboration (taking notes, iterating quickly, meeting deadlines).

title: Website Designer | Creative Portfolio Builder & Strategist
description:
Remote  |  Contract / Part-Time  | MINI Test Project to Start (use AI)



Type: Contract / Part-time (with potential to grow quickly within design branding team)

Location: Fully remote

Start: Paid test project if quality to level #2 candidate, then ongoing work if you crush it

Tools: Canva (required), AI tools (mandatory for design inspiration), Slack, Notion

Compensation: Salary with bonus (discussed after screening)

Team: 10+ Employees Across Canada/USA | 2+ VA's from Philippines | 5+ worldwide



>> Read this before you apply >  About Wovvo

Wovvo is a UGC platform built by two founders who got tired of watching talented creators struggle to find paid work. We’re bootstrapping in public, and we’re building the go-to destination for creators who want real brand deals, with a strong branded portfolio to go with it.



Part of our mission: helping creators present themselves like professionals on their Canva website (UGC portfolio). That’s where you come in.



>>  What You’ll Actually Do

You’ll be redesigning UGC creator portfolios from the ground up. Sometimes it’s re-vamp where the creator provides portfolio. Other times it’s from scratch. It’s not just making them look nice, but making them convert. Every portfolio you work on should answer one question for the brand looking at it: “Why should I hire this creator?”



>>  Day-to-day, that looks like:

Auditing existing creator portfolios and identifying what’s killing their conversion rate

Rebuilding portfolio structure, messaging, visual hierarchy, and credibility signals

Creating high-end portfolio designs in Canva that look custom, not 2016 style

Designing compelling case study sections, testimonial layouts, and video showcases

Using AI tools to speed up your workflow without sacrificing quality or originality

Use Canva features to make high-end quality website that reflects the creators personal brand

Turning around polished, brand-ready portfolios within tight timelines (target: ~7 hours per portfolio)



>>  This Role Is NOT For You If…

You think “good design” means picking a nice Canva template and swapping the text

You can’t explain WHY a design works, you just “feel” it

You’ve never thought about conversion, brand psychology, or what makes someone say yes

You need 3+ days to finish a single portfolio

You don’t use AI in your workflow (or don’t see the point)

You think “designer” and “strategist” are two different roles



>> This Role IS For You If…

You can look at a portfolio and immediately spot what’s holding it back

You understand that design is a tool for persuasion, not just aesthetics

You know how to make a portfolio look “high-end” and can articulate exactly what that means

You’re fast, structured, and can manage your own workflow without hand-holding

You use AI to accelerate your process, not replace your thinking

You’re genuinely excited about the creator economy and helping people land brand deals



>> Must-haves:

Advanced Canva skills (not beginner, not “I use templates” actually advanced)

Strong visual design eye:  typography, spacing, hierarchy, color

Portfolio or case study experience (show us work, not just a resume)

Understanding of conversion design:  how layout and messaging drive action

Experience with AI design tools (Midjourney, ChatGPT, or similar) as part of your process

Ability to work fast without sacrificing quality (~7 hour turnaround per portfolio)



>>  Bonus points:

Experience in the UGC or creator economy space

Understanding of what brands and agencies actually look for when hiring creators

Background in marketing, branding, or landing page design



----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



How We Evaluate

We’re scoring on 6 dimensions. Design ability is only one of them.





>> WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Design Thinking: Can you diagnose problems and prioritize fixes, not just make things look nice?



Conversion Awareness: Do you understand why a brand says yes or no? Do your changes drive action?



Creativity & Originality: Are your ideas fresh, or are they the same suggestions everyone gives?



Brand Psychology: Do you understand how credibility, trust, and positioning work visually?



Structure & Balance: Can you organize information so it flows logically and looks intentional?



Strategic Thinking: Do you think like a strategist who designs, or a designer who guesses?



>> Screening Questions

title: Client Success Coach – Earn Up to $2,500/Month
description:
Full-Time | Long-Term Role



Start at $935 per month base.

With bonuses, most earn around $1,100 per month.

Top performers grow to $1,800–$2,500 within 6–12 months.



Paid every two weeks via Wise. Always on time.



This is not a short-term job.

We build long-term careers.



You’ll have clear systems, call scripts, training, and daily team support.

You won’t be guessing what to do.



About Us



Young Publisher - 



We are an international digital marketing company.

We work with content creators and online brands.

We have hired Filipino professionals for over 5 years.

We focus on stability, structure, and growth.



What You’ll Gain



- Full-time stable income

- Monday to Friday schedule

- Flexible hours (one daily meeting required)

- No graveyard shifts

- Reliable payments via Wise every two weeks.

- Performance bonuses

- Clear path to increase your income

- All tools provided

- If you perform well, your income grows.



What You’ll Do



- Manage 40–50 clients

- Run onboarding calls (script provided)

- Review client content and give clear feedback

- Reply to messages within 24 hours

- Host weekly group calls and 1-on-1 sessions

- Track client progress and update reports

- Join daily team check-ins

- Your role is to help clients succeed and stay long-term.



What We’re Looking For



- Strong English (written and spoken)

- Confident on Zoom calls

- Organized and detail-oriented

- Reliable internet

- Quiet workspace

- Consistent and accountable



Bonus if you have experience with short-form content, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, or AI tools like ChatGPT and CapCut.



How to Apply

title: B2B Lead Scout (AI Visibility) – Legal & Real Estate
description:
We are a high-end AI Optimization agency helping professional firms (Law and Real Estate) capture the shift from Google Search to AI search (ChatGPT). We are looking for a Lead Scout to identify "invisible" firms and book them for a 15-minute Market Analysis.



Why this is a great role:



No Closing: You are only responsible for the initial 5-minute call and booking the appointment.

High Upside: We pay for performance. One close can net you a $100 bonus.

Vetted Leads: We provide the list; no manual lead generation required.



Compensation:



Base: $5.00 USD / hour.

The "Show" Bonus: $20.00 for every prospect that attends the 15-minute audit.

The "Close" Bonus: $100.00 for every deal closed from your appointments. 



Requirements:

Professional, neutral accent.

Experience with MyPhoner or similar power dialers.

Must be comfortable speaking with Law Firm Partners and Real Estate Brokers





How to Apply:

Please send a brief voice recording (under 60 seconds) introducing yourself. Tell us about your experience calling professional B2B firms.

title: Full-Stack Systems Architect & UI/UX Designer
description:
The Mission

Kinetic Marketing Co is a high-growth agency specializing in lead-generation "Engines" for high-ticket home transformation businesses (Construction, Renovations, Security). We don’t build "average" websites. We build premium, high-converting digital assets that position our clients as the #1 authority in their market.



We are looking for a Systems Architect who can blend technical GHL expertise with world-class UI/UX design across multiple platforms.



Key Responsibilities

Multi-Platform Development: Build and manage high-converting landing pages and websites across GoHighLevel (GHL), WordPress, and Shopify.



UI/UX Design: Create "Lambo-standard" designs that look premium and are optimized for 10/10 conversion rates.



Operational Workflows: Build and maintain complex backend automations (Workflows, Webhooks, API integrations via 
).



Agency Operations: Manage client onboarding technicals, DNS settings, and internal agency systems in ClickUp.



Collaboration: Work alongside the Director and Video Editor to ensure a seamless "Kinetic" brand experience across all funnels.



Requirements (The "Edge")

The Design Eye: You must have a portfolio that proves you can design high-end, modern layouts (Minimalist, bold, and professional).



Platform Mastery: Advanced knowledge of GHL (Snapshots, Custom Values, Proposall), WordPress (Elementor/Divi), and Shopify.



Technical Logic: You understand how to bridge data between different tools (CRMs,
, SMS).



Communication: Exceptional English (written and verbal) and a proactive "problem-solver" attitude.



Schedule: Ability to work 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM AEDT (Melbourne Time).



What We Offer

Full-Time Stability: 40 hours per week with a focus on long-term growth.



Professional Identity: You will be provided with a professional KMC
 and integrated into our leadership team.



Supportive Culture: We value high performance and provide the tools you need to succeed.



Fortnightly Pay: Consistent and reliable payments via Wise.



How to Apply

Please send your Portfolio (Direct links to GHL funnels, WordPress sites, or Shopify stores you have built).

Note: We are looking for custom-feel designs. If your portfolio is primarily stock templates, please do not apply.

title: Law Firm Receptionist
description:
We are a fast-moving U.S. law firm seeking an exceptionally sharp, emotionally intelligent, and highly reliable remote receptionist to become the first point of contact for our clients, courts, vendors, and legal teams.

This is not a passive answering role. You will serve as the voice and tone of the firm—handling sensitive conversations, directing urgent matters correctly, protecting attorney time, and ensuring every caller experiences professionalism, calm, and confidence.  We need someone who is highly intelligent, emotionally mature, fast with technology, and capable of reading situations with judgment.

    What You Will Do

Answer incoming calls professionally and confidently in fluent English

Handle emotionally sensitive callers with calm, empathy, and control

Route calls accurately to attorneys, paralegals, or departments

Schedule appointments and manage calendars precisely

Monitor voi
,
, and internal communication channels

Enter and update information in firm systems with accuracy

Follow communication protocols without missing details

Recognize urgency and escalate matters correctly

Maintain composure when clients are stressed, upset, or confused

Support attorneys and staff by protecting workflow and preventing interruptions

        Who We Are Looking For

You must be:

Extremely intelligent and quick to understand context

Highly emotionally intelligent (able to read tone, regulate difficult conversations, and remain composed)

Naturally organized and detail-driven

Excellent in spoken and written English

Confident speaking with professionals and distressed clients alike

Fast with software and willing to learn systems quickly

Able to think independently without constant supervision

Mature, discreet, and highly dependable

                  You Will Stand Out If You Have

Experience in a law firm, medical office, executive office, or high-pressure client service role or in hospitality field.

Experience handling U.S. clients by phone

Strong scheduling discipline

CRM or case-management software experience

Excellent listening skills and strong memory

           This Role Is NOT for Someone Who

Waits to be told every next step

Becomes overwhelmed easily

Misses details

Struggles with difficult conversations

Has weak English communication

Technical Requirements

Quiet professional workspace

Stable high-speed internet

Reliable computer

Noise-cancelling headset

Camera-ready for internal meetings.  We need dependable people only. Not a part time position.

title: ???? Rockstar Customer Service & Dispatch Coordinator Needed, High Growth Role
description:
???? Rockstar Customer Service & Dispatch Coordinator Needed, High Growth Role



We are not looking for a typical VA.



We are building a fast-growing service company in Florida, and we need someone who can operate at a high level, think fast, and handle real responsibility.



If you are organized, detail-oriented, and great with customers, this could turn into a long-term opportunity.





About Us:



We are Palmway Pools, a premium pool service company that services residential homes and Airbnb properties.



Our customers rely on us to keep their pools in perfect condition, especially when guests are staying at their homes.



That means communication, speed, and attention to detail matter at a very high level.







What You’ll Be Doing:



You will be the bridge between customers and our field technicians.



This is not just customer support, this is operations.



* Answer inbound calls and messages from customers

* Coordinate and dispatch technicians efficiently

* Handle scheduling, rescheduling, and urgent issues

* Communicate clearly with both customers and the field team

* Track jobs and ensure nothing falls through the cracks

* Follow up with customers to ensure satisfaction

* Keep everything organized and documented





 Tools You’ll Be Using:



* WhatsApp

* Slack

* Go High Level (GHL)



What We’re Looking For:



We are looking for someone sharp.



* Excellent English, both written and spoken

* Experience in a service-based business (home services is a big plus)

* Strong attention to detail, you catch things others miss

* Fast thinker, able to handle pressure and make decisions

* Highly organized and proactive

* Great with customers, calm, professional, and confident

* Reliable and responsive, we move fast







This Role Is NOT For You If:



* You need constant supervision

* You miss details or forget tasks

* You are slow to respond

* You struggle with handling multiple things at once





## This Role IS For You If:



* You take ownership and solve problems

* You like being busy and moving fast

* You want to grow with a company long term

* You care about doing things right the first time





 Growth Opportunity:



This is a performance-based role.



If you perform at a high level, there will be opportunities for increased pay and more responsibility over time.



We reward reliability, speed, and attention to detail.





How to Apply:



To apply, send a message with:



1. A short introduction about yourself

2. Your experience with customer service and dispatching

3. A voice recording (30 to 60 seconds) explaining why you would be a good fit

4. The word **Palmway** at the top of your application so we know you read everything





If you are sharp, organized, and ready to grow, we want to hear from you.

title: Senior Service Desk / Helpdesk Manager (Higher Education)
description:
• Reports to Senior Consultant, Digital Transformation

• Permanent, Full-Time

• Client-facing role

• Excellent English (essential)

• Philippines-based, corporate leadership background preferred

• Critical thinker with proven problem-solving ability



About the role



We are looking for someone to join our International Team as a Senior Service / HelpDesk Manager (Service Delivery Manager) for our client projects. This role will sit within our Digital Transformation business line, supporting universities and higher education providers to deliver exceptional post-implementation and operational support.



In this role, you will be accountable for managing the transition of technology projects into operational support, delivering high-quality service to clients, and driving ongoing improvements across client engagements.



You will be working across complex, multi-stakeholder environments, with a strong focus on managing i
, request, change, and escalation processes/ You’ll take ownership of major i
, coordinate resolution activities, monitor performance, and continuously identify opportunities for service improvement, ensuring SLAs and client expectations are met.





Key responsibilities:



• Accountable for high quality service support functions

• Ownership of I
, Request, Change and Escalation processes, ensuring timely response, accurate reporting and establishing service improvement activities when required

• Manage the helpdesk function, ensuring support tickets are triaged, assigned, resolved, and closed within SLA.

• Take ownership of major i
, ensuring coordination of resolving parties, effective communication to stakeholders and post i
 review

• Monitor, control and support service delivery, ensuring systems, methodologies and procedures are in place and followed

• Ensure a strong understanding of technology projects is developed so that service transition impact is known and planned for

• Accountable for the quality of service and performance; ensuring future demand from growth and projects is understood and factored into capacity plans

• Creation of standard operating procedures for key service delivery functions

• Become the trusted advisor of customers by effectively understanding key goals and strategizing with Service Delivery team to support and ensure customer success

• Manage stakeholder communication and expectation management

• Work with delivery teams to ensure meeting of SLA’s and standard turn-around times





What we are looking for (selection criteria):



• Proven experience managing end-to-end service delivery or technology projects, including meeting hard deadlines and delivering to scope.

• Strong background in helpdesk or service desk operations, with experience triaging and resolving support tickets in a client-facing environment.

• Familiarity with cloud-based software or SaaS platforms, ideally in a support or implementation role.

• Fluent in English as you will be the primary point of contact for clients, partners, and coordinating the work with our internal team. You should be able to effectively communicate requirements, timelines, etc. Experience in collaborating with clients and partners to achieve project goals is important

• Highly organised, able to manage competing priorities and meet strict deadlines without compromising quality and thrives in a fast-paced environment. You need to ensure that activities are tracking to plan and schedules, and deliverables are submitted on time without compromising quality.

• Demonstrated success in managing mid-to-large projects, with a proven ability to deliver on-time and within scope.

• Comfortable handling complex or escalated service issues and driving them to resolution.

• Able to roll their sleeves up and do the leg work in key project activities.





Desired (but not essential):



• Experience working with higher education institutions or supporting education-focused IT systems.

• Exposure to transitioning technology projects into managed service environments

• Familiarity with ITIL or similar service management frameworks

• Experience supporting cloud-based SaaS tools or platform deployments

• Experience working with international teams





Other Requirements:



• A modern computer with minimum specs: i5 processor or equivalent, 8GB RAM (ideally 16GB), 250GB HD and must support and capable of running software used by DVE including Microsoft 365 suite, Microsoft Visio

• Fibre, DSL or Cable internet as your main internet connection (stable) with internet speed of at least 150Mbps

• A backup 4G/LTE/5G internet connection (could be a Wi-Fi dongle or pocket Wi-Fi)

• Conducive work environment and home office setup

• Chrome browser, Adobe Acrobat reader

• Genuine Windows OS installation - some internal software we use do not work with MacOS

• UPS/backup generator (preferred)





Some of the reasons why you should be a member of the DVE Team:



• Full-time, long-term contract

• Ability to work from home and not have to commute to and from work

• Experience working with an Australian company

• Challenging and rewarding, with opportunities to progress and grow your role and career

• Work with a great team that supports you.

• HMO and leave benefits

• Opportunity to receive periodic coaching from senior tea
mbers





About DVE Solutions



DVE Business Solutions is a growing consulting company with a difference, led by South Australia Telstra Young Business Woman of the Year Jo Schneider. DVE clients are predominantly in the education sector with Universities and Higher Ed providers being core focus. With our communicative and collaborative methodology, DVE drives positive change that makes a difference in the areas of People, Process, Structure and Technology.



We work every day to change education from the inside out, and our work is underpinned by our core values - Work as OneTeam; Practice what we preach; Maintain client focus and; Enjoy what we do.

mbers

title: Recruitment Support Specialist. (Admin & Compliance experience required)
description:
Admin & Recruitment Support Specialist

Full-Time | Remote | Australian Recruitment Company



We are an Australian-based BPO, seeking an experienced Admin and Recruitment Support specialist for our client, an Australian recruitment company. This full time (FT) role is 40 hours per week, during Australian business hours 9am-5pm AEST. Offering the flexibility of working-from-home (WFH) and a market leading salary package + paid leave + bonuses. 



This is a fast-paced, multi-functional role supporting administration, recruitment, marketing, and compliance. We’re looking for someone highly organized, proactive, reliable, and confident working across multiple systems and stakeholders.



*****Key Responsibilities Include*****



General Administration

• Manage LinkedIn activity for a senior tea
mber 

• Respond to daily internal admin queries 

• Answer incoming calls via Aircall 

• Take minutes in weekly company meetings 

• Track internal events (EOFY awards, birthdays, anniversaries, team recognition) 

• Raise and follow up system tickets (Bullhorn / Astute) 

• Support internal requests (employment letters, account confirmations, super follow-ups) 

• Attend tea
etings and align on priorities 



Recruitment Admin & Compliance

• Format candidate CVs using ReMake 

• Conduct reference checks (phone + Referoo) 

• Complete VEVO checks and validate visa information in Astute 

• Maintain and update candidate records in Bullhorn (BH) 

• Manage onboarding support and troubleshooting 

• Handle time-sensitive onboarding queries 

• Send and update Confirmation of Assignment details 

• Track compliance items (police checks, references, NUR forms) 

• Manage job ad postings and updates 

• Maintain Care Team inbox and candidate communication 

• Support temp placement approvals and documentation 

• Prepare employment verification letters 



Marketing Support

• Send new starter “get to know you” communications 

• Create basic graphics using Canva 

• Assist with presentations 

• Manage testimonials and website updates 

• Support internal campaigns (e.g. Life Saver of the Month) 

• Prepare welcome packs for new starters 



Reporting, Compliance & Analytics

• Prepare monthly compliance and placement reports 

• Generate reports from Astute and Bullhorn Analytics 

• Track team and individual targets 

• Produce client and internal performance reports 

• Support compliance tracking and approvals 

• Assist with data accuracy and system integrations 



*****Systems & Tools You’ll Use*****

• Bullhorn (CRM) 

• Astute 

• Referoo 

• Aircall 

• ReMake (CV formatting tool) 

• Canva 

• LinkedIn 

• Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) 



***** Key Requirements Include ***** MUST HAVES to apply



• At least 2 years previous experience in recruitment admin, HR admin, or high-volume admin support. 

• At least 2 years previous experience with compliance and onboarding for recruitment. 

• High level of proficiency with LinkedIN/ LinkedIN Recruiter.

• High level of proficiency with Bullhorn. ATS and CRM.

• Previous experience working with international clients, AUS, US or UK. 

• Strong attention to detail and organizational skills 

• Excellent written and spoken English. 

• Confidence speaking to clients and candidates over the phone. 

• Ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines 

• Comfortable working across multiple systems 



IDEAL CANDIDATES have

• Previous experience in an Australian recruitment agency environment 

• Familiarity with Astute 



*****Important – How To Apply*****



To be considered, please follow these instructions:

1. Link your CV for us to review. 

2. Provide an overview of your WFH set-up. ( You must have your own work device, fast and reliable internet, back-up internet and power options, co-working space/internet cafe within 20 minutes travel time). 

3. Provide a short outline of your experience in recruitment/ admin support/ compliance. 

4. List the systems/tools you’ve used (e.g. Bullhorn, CRM, etc.) 

5. Provide a short video/voice introduction to yourself. 



Applications that do not follow instructions may not be reviewed.



Thank you

title: GoHighLevel Specialist / CRM & Automation VA
description:
About Us

Kinetic Marketing Co is a Melbourne-based digital marketing agency specialising in lead generation, CRM automation, and growth systems for trades businesses and home improvement contractors across Australia. We run Meta Ads, Google Ads, and build end-to-end client systems using GoHighLevel. We're a fast-moving agency and we need someone who can keep up.



The Role

We're looking for a GoHighLevel specialist who can hit the ground running. You'll be responsible for building, managing, and optimising client accounts inside GHL — from pipeline setup through to full automation workflows. This is a hands-on execution role. We don't have time to train someone from scratch — we need someone who already knows GHL deeply and can take a brief and deliver without constant hand-holding.



What You'll Be Doing



-Building and managing GHL sub-accounts for new and existing clients

-Setting up pipelines, stages, and opportunity management

-Building automation workflows — SMS,
, task triggers, lead nurture sequences

-Setting up and customising landing pages and funnels inside GHL

-Configuring lead forms, calendars, and booking systems

-Connecting integrations (Zapier, Webhooks, third-party tools)

-Building and managing GoHighLevel snapshots

-Troubleshooting automations and client account issues

-Communicating build progress clearly with the team

-Bonus: WordPress website builds for clients who require it





What We're Looking For

Non-negotiable:



-Minimum 2 years hands-on GoHighLevel experience

-Strong understanding of automation logic and CRM workflows

-Able to work independently with minimal supervision

-Excellent written English communication

-Detail-oriented — you check your own work before submitting

-Responsive and proactive — you raise issues before they become problems

-Available to overlap with Australian business hours (AEST)



Highly regarded:



-WordPress build experience (Elementor or similar)

-Experience working with trades or home service businesses

-Familiarity with Meta Ads lead form integrations into GHL

-Experience building client-facing reporting or dashboards





How to Apply

To be considered you must include the following in your application:



A short Loom video (3–5 minutes) walking us through a GoHighLevel build you have completed — show us a workflow, pipeline, or automation you're proud of



A brief summary of your GHL experience and the types of clients/industries you've worked with

Your availability and preferred hourly rate in USD



Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed.

We're hiring immediately. If you're the right person, you'll be onboarded fast.

title: Landscape Designer (Vectorworks Proficiencies)
description:
Position Description: Virtual Assistant – Landscape Construction Drawings & 3D Rendering

About the Role



We are seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to support our landscape design studio in delivering high-quality construction documentation and 3D visualisations.



This role is suited to someone with strong technical expertise in Vectorworks and Enscape, who can confidently translate design intent into clear, buildable drawings and high-end visual renders.



You will work closely with the lead designer to bring concepts to life, ensuring projects are both visually compelling and accurately documented for construction.



Key Responsibilities

Construction Documentation (Vectorworks)

Produce detailed landscape construction drawing sets using Vectorworks

Prepare plans including:

Layout and set-out plans

Levels and drainage plans

Planting plans

Lighting and services plans

Construction details and sections

Ensure all drawings are clear, accurate, and ready for construction

Maintain consistent studio standards, including classes, layers, line weights, and sheet layouts

Implement revisions efficiently based on markups and feedback

3D Rendering & Visualisation (Enscape)

Build and refine 3D models within Vectorworks

Produce high-quality renders using Enscape

Apply realistic materials, lighting, and planting to reflect design intent

Create still renders and, where required, simple walkthroughs

Ensure all visual outputs align with the studio’s aesthetic and quality expectations



Collaboration & Workflow

Interpret sketches, markups, and verbal direction from the lead designer

Communicate clearly around timelines, progress, and deliverables

Manage multiple projects and deadlines efficiently

Maintain organised file structures, naming conventions, and version control



Required Skills & Experience

Advanced proficiency in Vectorworks (essential)

Strong experience using Enscape for rendering (essential)

Proven experience producing construction-ready documentation

Strong understanding of:

Landscape construction methods

Materials and detailing

Levels, drainage, and set-out principles

High attention to detail and accuracy

Ability to work from sketches, PDFs, and design markups



Preferred (Not Essential)

Experience working on Australian residential landscape projects

Understanding of local construction standards and materials

Experience with planting plans and schedules

Background in landscape architecture, architecture, or horticulture



Working Style & Expectations

Remote, flexible working arrangement

Reliable availability for communication and feedback

Ability to work independently while taking clear direction

Fast turnaround times with a strong focus on quality

Open to feedback and iterative design refinement



Key Outcomes

High-quality, buildable construction drawing sets delivered in Vectorworks

Realistic, high-end 3D renders produced through Enscape

Consistent documentation and visual output aligned with studio standards

Efficient support across multiple projects



About the Studio



Poa Landscape Design is a Melbourne-based studio focused on creating modern, native landscapes that are both functional and highly considered. Our work blends natural materials with strong design intent to create spaces that feel calm, immersive, and connected to place.

Client Communication

Respond to client queries within 1 working day

Communicate clearly and professionally

Keep communication simple and commercially aware

Escalate anything outside your level of expertise



Data Accuracy & Cleanliness

Maintain clean ledgers with no duplication or miscoding

Ensure all accounts are fully reconciled

Clear suspense accounts promptly

Monitor aged debtors and creditors

Regularly review bank feeds and financial data



Escalation & Awareness

Identify and flag unusual transactions

Highlight compliance risks or inconsistencies

Raise cash flow concerns where relevant

Escalate anything unclear or outside your scope



Reporting

Provide weekly updates on:



Work completed

Work in progress

Missing information

Risks or issues



Essential Skills & Experience

Strong experience using QuickBooks Online (non-negotiable)

Proven experience in bookkeeping or accountancy support

Strong understanding of reconciliations, VAT, and ledgers

High level of accuracy and attention to detail

Strong English communication skills

Able to manage workload independently

Organised and process-driven



What We’re Looking For

We want someone who:



Takes full ownership of their work

Keeps financial records clean, accurate, and review-ready

Works proactively (not reactively)

Manages deadlines without being chased

Communicates clearly with both clients and internal team

Spots issues early and raises them



What This Role Isn’t

Not basic data entry

Not leaving reconciliations unfinished

Not allowing backlog to build

Not ignoring errors or inconsistencies



How to Apply:

Use “Vixa Hire” in the subject line of your application.

Send your resume and cover letter.

Include your internet speed test result and a screenshot of your laptop specs.



If you’re a detail-driven QuickBooks specialist who takes pride in clean, accurate bookkeeping, this is a great opportunity to take ownership within a growing UK accountancy firm.

title: VA COLD CALLER
description:
**FULL-TIME VIRTUAL ASSISTANT – COLD CALLER & OPERATIONS | Marketing Agency | USA & CANADA MARKET**



We are a growing marketing agency working with construction companies across the United States and Canada. We are looking for a highly motivated, reliable, and fluent English-speaking Virtual Assistant to join our team full-time.



This is a long-term position with real earning potential. We reward performance.



---



**ABOUT THE ROLE:**



Your main job is to call 100 construction businesses per day in the USA and Canada, follow our proven script, qualify leads, and pass interested prospects directly to our closer. You will also handle follow-up and basic operations tasks during the rest of your shift.



---



**YOUR DAILY RESPONSIBILITIES:**



???? Cold Calling (5 hours/day)

- Call 100 businesses per day from a list we provide

- follow or if you have ideas the its fine improvising if  needed

- Identify the decision maker (owner or manager)

- Qualify leads based on criteria we provide

- Immediately notify the closer via WhatsApp when a prospect shows interest

- Log every call in our CRM: answered / not answered / proposal sent / interested / not interested / callback requested



???? Operations (3 hours/day)

- Send follow-up
s or Instagram DMs to leads that didn't answer

- Help grow and organize the prospect list using Google Maps and free tools

- Keep CRM updated with accurate notes after every call

- Send a daily end-of-shift report including: total calls made, interested leads, follow-ups sent, callbacks scheduled



---



**REQUIREMENTS:**



? Fluent English neutral or North American accent preferred, clear and confident speaking voice (non-negotiable)

? Minimum 6 months of cold calling or phone sales experience in the US or Canadian market

? Good quality headset and microphone

? Available Monday to Friday, 8 hours/day during North American business hours (EST or PST) — note: this will require night shift in the Philippines

? Comfortable using Google Sheets, WhatsApp, and basic CRM tools

? Self-managed, honest, and punctual we don't micromanage but we track results daily



---



**COMPENSATION:**



???? Base salary: $300/month  paid weekly via Wise or PayPal

???? Performance bonus:

- $5 for every qualified lead that books a call with our closer

- $25–$50 for every client that signs a contract from your calls

- Top performers can earn an additional $200–$500/month on top of base salary



???? Stable long-term position  we grow, you grow



---



**HOW TO APPLY:**



Do NOT just send your resume. Generic applications will be ignored immediately.



To be considered you MUST include ALL of the following:



1. ? A 2-minute voice recording in English where you introduce yourself AS IF you were making a real cold call to a construction company owner in Canada or the USA (follow this structure: introduction ? reason for calling ? qualifying question ? close for appointment)

2. ? Your resume or a short summary of your relevant experience

3. ? Written answers to these 3 questions:

   - How many calls per day did you make in your last role?

   - What country/market were you calling?

   - What was your average conversion rate from calls to appointments?



Applications missing the voice recording or the 3 questions will not be reviewed — no exceptions.



---



We are hiring immediately. Only serious, experienced candidates apply.

title: Chief Operating Officer (COO) / Integrator for Scaling U.S. Law Firm (Remote)
description:
We are a growing U.S.-based law firm seeking a highly seasoned, operationally elite Chief Operating Officer to take full command of firm operations and help scale intelligently, profitably, and with discipline.

This role requires a true operator—someone who quickly sees inefficiencies, identifies revenue leaks, imposes accountability, and builds systems that allow a founder-led firm to operate at a significantly higher level.

You must think like an owner, act with discretion, and execute with speed.

This is not an assistant role. This is a senior leadership position responsible for turning a successful legal practice into a highly organized, scalable operating company.

Core Mission

Drive operational excellence across all departments while increasing profitability, reducing waste, improving production efficiency, and building the systems required for scale.

You Will Own

Full law firm operations oversight

Build and enforce systems, SOPs, workflows, and accountability

Implement and manage EOS across the organization

Design and monitor dashboards for performance, KPIs, profitability, and bottlenecks

Oversee intake, sales process, and conversion systems

Build or optimize the sales department

Monitor marketing spend, vendor performance, CAC, ROI, and lead quality

Improve staffing structure and productivity

Reduce overhead and eliminate inefficiencies

Organize internal file systems, shared drives, and information architecture

Standardize internal communication and execution discipline

Create operational visibility across attorneys, staff, billing, and client lifecycle

Work directly with founder to convert strategic priorities into execution

Strong Preference

Prior COO / Integrator experience inside a law firm

Family law / divorce law operational understanding strongly preferred

Experience with legal intake systems, retainers, collections, attorney production, and client lifecycle

Experience scaling professional services firms

Required Competencies

Strong financial intelligence (margin, ROI, overhead control, production cost analysis)

Ability to quickly diagnose organizational dysfunction

Strong systems mind

Deep accountability orientation

Exceptional judgment

High trustworthiness and discretion

Strong leadership without unnecessary ego

Ability to enter complexity and create order quickly

Systems Experience Preferred

PracticePanther

GoHighLevel

Airtable

Google Workspace

EOS scorecards / meeting cadence / accountability charts

What Success Looks Like in First 6 Months

Clear organizational structure

Department scorecards live

Sales department functioning with measurable close ratios

Marketing costs tied to case-type profitability

SOP library operational

Waste reduced

Founder no longer trapped in daily operational friction

Revenue visibility by department and case type

This Role Is Not For Someone Who

Needs constant direction

Is theoretical but slow to execute

Cannot challenge inefficiency

Avoids hard accountability

Has never owned outcomes

To Apply

Submit:

Resume

Detailed summary of firms or businesses you have operationally scaled

One example where you materially increased profitability

One example where you reduced overhead or rebuilt systems

Short video explaining how you would approach a founder-led law firm with inconsistent systems and rapid growth.  Compensation based on experience and proven executive ability. Starting range: $3,000–$4,000 USD monthly, with performance-based increases tied to measurable operational impact.

title: Marketing Agency Operations & Content VA
description:
Job Type: Full-Time | Remote

Schedule: Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM PST

Salary: $1,000 – $1,500/month (based on experience)



About the Role

We are looking for an operationally sharp, tech-forward Virtual Assistant to join our team. This role supports a fast-paced digital marketing environment where you will handle project management, content scheduling, inbox management, and client coordination. No two weeks look exactly alike.

This is not a generalist VA role. You will be deeply embedded in day-to-day operations and are expected to hit the ground running from day one.



What You'll Do



Manage client tasks, folders, and project documentation in Teamwork

Track follow-ups and deadlines across multiple client accounts

Set up and maintain client content calendars in Google Sheets

Schedule weekly blogs in WordPress (WP Engine) including image selection and upload

Schedule and manage social media posts across platforms using Metricool

Prepare and distribute client meeting agendas

Manage the executive inbox and general support inbox

Coordinate team scheduling and calendar management

Maintain organized client folders in Google Drive

Review and lightly edit AI-generated blog content for quality and consistency

Use ChatGPT and other AI tools daily to improve speed and accuracy

Support creative tasks such as Canva thumbnail creation





Requirements



Requirements



Hands-on experience with Teamwork, Asana, or 

Experience with Metricool or a strong alternative (Buffer, Hootsuite)

Prior experience working inside a marketing or creative agency

Fluent, professional English with exceptional written accuracy

Strong Google Drive proficiency (Docs and Sheets)

Comfortable using AI tools (ChatGPT or similar) as part of daily work

Available Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM–5:00 PM PST without schedule conflict

Fast, accurate typist with strong attention to detail





Bonus Points



HubSpot newsletter experience

Adobe Acrobat / PDF editing

WordPress or WP Engine experience

Eye for design and on-brand image selection

Canva graphics experience





Compensation & Benefits



$1,000 – $1,500/month based on experience

Bi-weekly pay

15 paid days off annually

Performance-based bonuses

Fully remote, long-term role





Hiring Process

Shortlisted candidates will be asked to complete a paid 3-hour skills assessment before a final decision is made. A short 1-minute video introduction is also required with your application.



What Success Looks Like

Projects stay on track without reminders. Client content goes out accurately and on time. You don't wait to be told what to do next — you keep the engine running.

No yelling, no drama. Just real work with real people who want you to succeed.



To Apply

Send your updated resume and a 1-minute video introduction (Loom or similar) telling us your name, your experience with Teamwork, Asana, or 
 and Metricool (or similar tools), and why this role is a great fit for you.

title: Credit Repair Specialist – Disputes & Compliance
description:
We are seeking an experienced Credit Repair Specialist to work alongside our company and manage credit repair clients from start to finish.



This is not a beginner role. We are looking for someone who understands consumer law, compliance, and can confidently execute dispute strategies with accuracy and professionalism.



???? Responsibilities:

 • Analyze full credit reports and identify negative or inaccurate items

 • Develop and execute customized dispute strategies

 • Handle disputes with all three credit bureaus and creditors

 • Submit complaints through CFPB and FTC when appropriate

 • Utilize factual-based disputing methods (no template-only approaches)

 • Maintain organized client files and provide consistent progress updates

 • Ensure all work remains fully compliant with FCRA and consumer protection laws



???? Requirements:

 • Proven, hands-on experience in credit repair (required)

 • Strong working knowledge of:

 • FCRA (Fair Credit Reporting Act)

 • FTC regulations

 • CFPB complaint process

 • Ability to create effective, strategic dispute letters (not generic templates)

 • Clear understanding of compliance and legal boundaries within credit repair

 • Strong organization, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple clients





???? Bonus Skills:

 • Experience working within a credit repair company or agency

 • Knowledge of Metro 2 and credit reporting systems

 • Familiarity with business credit



???? Position Details:

 • Remote position

 • Part-time to start (opportunity for growth)

 • Must be reliable, responsive, and results-driven



???? How to Apply:



Please submit:

 1. A summary of your credit repair experience

 2. Examples of dispute strategies or results (if available)

 3. A brief explanation of how you approach negative items on a credit report



???? Application Task (Required)



To be considered, complete the following:



1. Scenario:

A client has:

 • 2 collections

 • 1 charge-off

 • 3 late payments



 Explain your step-by-step strategy for handling these items.



2. Dispute Approach:

 Describe how you structure your disputes (factual vs. other methods).



3. Compliance:

 Name 2–3 things you should NOT say or promise to a client.





?? Important:



We are looking for someone who can produce results while remaining compliant. If you do not have real experience with consumer law and dispute handling, this position is not a fit.



Applications without the completed task will not be considered.



Serious applicants only. Include the word ‘COMPLIANCE’ in your response to be considered.

title: Video Editor
description:
About Tend Productions:



Tend Productions is a video production company based in Austin, Texas. We produce content for a diverse roster of clients across industries including combat sports, food and restaurant, real estate, CPG brands, and more. Our work spans short-form social content, branded commercial videos, cinematic food and lifestyle content, and paid ad creative.

We’re looking for a talented video editor to join us on a part-time basis and grow with the company. This is not a one-off gig — it’s an opportunity to become an integral part of a small, fast-moving production team where your work directly impacts the brand and bottom line for real clients.



What You’ll Do:



- Edit raw footage (sometimes SLOG3) into polished final deliverables based on detailed creative briefs

- Create short-form social content (Reels, TikToks, YouTube Shorts) optimized for engagement and platform best practices

- Edit listing videos, branded content, cinematic food videos, and personality-driven content across multiple clients

- Color correct and grade footage to match each client’s brand standards

- Mix audio, add music, and incorporate sound design to elevate edits

- Create and apply text overlays and motion graphics

- Reformat and repurpose long-form content into multiple platform-specific cuts (16:9, 9:16, 1:1, 4:5)

- Manage and organize project files in shared cloud storage (Google Drive)

- Communicate proactively on timelines, questions, and revision status



Who You Are:



Required

- Fluent in English — written and spoken. You’ll be on weekly video calls and communicating semi-daily via messaging

- Proficient in Adobe Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve

- 2+ years of professional video editing experience with a portfolio or reel you can share

- Ability to edit for multiple different brands

- Strong understanding of pacing, storytelling, and what makes short-form content perform on social platforms

- Solid color correction and audio mixing skills

- Reliable high-speed internet and your own editing workstation capable of handling 4K footage

- Available for a weekly sync call during U.S. Central Time business hours (flexible on exact time)

- Self-motivated and organized — you can manage your own schedule and meet deadlines without being micromanaged



Nice to Have:



- After Effects skills for motion graphics, transitions, and animated text

- Experience editing food/restaurant content, real estate listing videos, or combat sports highlights

- Familiarity with social media trends, hooks, and platform-specific best practices (TikTok, Instagram, YouTube Shorts)

- Experience working with creative briefs and feedback/revision workflows

- Basic sound design and SFX skills

title: Senior Para Broker / Credit Analyst – Australian Mortgage Broking
description:
Para Broker / Credit Analyst

Australian Mortgage Broking – Complex & Medical Lending

Applicants must have minimum 3 years experience analysing Australian mortgage lending scenarios.



Full-Time | Permanent | Work From Home

Salary: 70,000 – 100,000 PHP per month depending on experience



This Role Is For a Real Credit Analyst — Not a Broker Admin



We are an established Australian mortgage brokerage specialising in lending for medical professionals and complex borrowers. Our work regularly involves residential, investment, commercial, SMSF, trust structures, and high-net-worth lending.



We are looking for a genuinely experienced Para Broker or Credit Analyst who understands how to structure lending, interpret credit policy, and produce strong lender-ready applications.



This role is not suitable for applicants whose experience is primarily document collection, CRM updates, application submission only, or broker administration.



If your previous role mainly involved preparing files for submission or acting as broker support, this position will not be the right fit.



The Type of Analyst We Are Looking For



You are someone who can think like a lender, not just process paperwork.



You have experience assessing borrowing capacity and servicing, identifying suitable lenders based on credit policy, structuring deals involving trusts, SMSF or commercial borrowers, and preparing clear credit submissions that help lenders approve deals.



You enjoy solving problems and working on lending scenarios where every file is different.



Mandatory Experience Requirements



Please only apply if you meet all of the following requirements.



You must have at least 3 years experience as a Para Broker or Credit Analyst in Australian mortgage broking.



You must have experience analysing and structuring residential and investment lending.



Exposure to commercial lending, trust lending or SMSF lending is highly desirable.



You must have strong knowledge of Australian lender credit policy and excellent written and spoken English.



You must also have experience using the following platforms:



ApplyOnline (AOL)

Mercury CRM

Quickli



If you are unfamiliar with these systems, you are not qualified for this role.



What You Will Be Doing



Analysing client financials and servicing positions



Researching lender credit policy and structuring lending solutions



Recommending lenders and loan strategies to brokers



Preparing high quality credit submissions



Assisting with complex lending scenarios including SMSF and trust structures



Supporting files through to approval and settlement



You will work closely with experienced brokers who handle complex transactions, so your work will directly influence deal outcomes.



Why This Role Is Different



Many offshore roles in mortgage broking are administrative.



This role is not.



You will be working with brokers who specialise in complex and high-value lending, including medical professional lending, SMSF lending, trust lending, commercial lending, and high-net-worth borrowers.



We value intelligence, initiative, strong analytical ability and attention to detail.



If you enjoy working on challenging deals and learning advanced lending strategy, you will thrive in this environment.



What We Offer



Permanent full-time work from home role



Monday to Friday, 9am to 6pm AEST



Salary between 70,000 and 100,000 PHP depending on experience



A small experienced team of high-performing brokers and analysts



Exposure to complex lending scenarios most analysts never see



We are building a long-term team and are looking for someone who wants to grow with the business.



Application Instructions (Important)



Please do not send a CV only.



Applications without the following will not be reviewed.



Record a short video introduction (2 to 4 minutes) where you:



Explain your experience as a Credit Analyst or Para Broker



Describe the types of lending scenarios you have worked on



Explain how your role differed from broker administration or loan processing



Demonstrate your spoken English communication



Upload the video to Google Drive or YouTube (unlisted) and include the link in your application.



Failure to follow these instructions will result in automatic rejection as attention to detail is critical in this role.



If you are an experienced credit analyst who enjoys problem-solving, complex lending structures and working with high-performing professionals, we would like to hear from you.

title: Para-Broker / Mortgage Broking Credit Analyst
description:
Para Broker - Credit Analyst – Australian Mortgage Broking (Medical & Complex Lending Specialist)

Work from Home | Full-Time | 60,000 - 75,000 PHP pm



Are you an EXPERIENCED / SENIOR Para-broker or Credit Analyst ready to level up your career and be part of a thriving, high-performing team? We're an established mortgage brokerage based in Australia, specialising in Finance for Medical professionals — but we handle residential (owner-occupied and investment), commercial, SMSF, complex, and high-net-worth lending.



We're not your average brokerage. Every day is different. Every file presents a new challenge. Our clients are driven, intelligent professionals — and they expect the same from us.



That’s why we’re looking for a **GENUINLY EXPERIENCED** Credit Analyst / Para - Broker — not someone who ONLY submits applications via AOL or plays a broker support/admin role.



What We’re Looking For:



End-to-end experience: From researching borrowing structures, assessing servicing, recommending lenders, and preparing strong applications — through to following deals to settlement.



Confident across multiple loan types: You’ve worked on residential, investment, commercial, SMSF, and trusts, and you're comfortable navigating credit policy across major banks and non-bank lenders.



EXCELLENT English communication skills: You’ll liaise directly with brokers and sometimes clients — explaining your recommendations clearly and concisely.



Problem-solving mindset: You enjoy working through complex deals and helping find smart, viable solutions.



Not a support role: If your background is primarily in administration or document collection/submission only, this role is NOT suitable.



A successful applicant will have experience working with AOL, Mercury & Quickly. If you do not know what these programs are, then you are NOT qualified to enquire for this role. 



Why Join Us?



* Work with a tight-knit team of 3 brokers and 3 support staff who LOVE what they do

* We have a 100% approval track record and highly satisfied clients — your work will directly impact real outcomes

* Above-market salary for the right candidate — we pay well and value skill

* A fun, fast-paced culture — every day is different, and we keep things exciting

* Full-time, permanent, work-from-home role: Monday to Friday, 9am to 6pm AEST





How to Apply



Please DO NOT send a CV only.



We want to hear from you.

Send us a short VIDEO INTRODUCTION  (2–4 minutes) where you:



* Walk us through your experience as a Credit Analyst

* Explain the types of loans and clients you’ve worked with

* Tell us how your role differs from broker support or admin staff

* Show us your communication style and English fluency



Upload your video to Google Drive or YouTube (unlisted) and include the link in your application. Failure to do this will result in AUTOMATIC DECLINE of your application, as it will show a lack of attention to detail. ATTENTION TO DETAIL is KEY to this role.  



If you're a true credit analyst who loves problem-solving, has a high attention to detail, excellent English, thrives in a high-achieving team, and wants to work on complex deals that challenge you — this is your next great opportunity.



We look forward to seeing your video!
